info.name: Truora Collection
info.description: Truora API Team: [developers@truora.com](https://mailto:developers@truora.com) You’ve found Truora's Collection! Dedicated to showing you all the ways that you can use Truora’s products from the API. Truora’s API is organized around REST. Our API has predictable resource-oriented URLs, accepts form-encoded request bodies, returns JSON-encoded responses, and uses standard HTTP response codes, authentication, and verbs. The following steps allow you to configure the collection appropriately for your use: ## Prerequisites - Truora Account > [API Key](https://dev.truora.com/guides/authentication/index.html) ## **Let's start** ### Step 1: Export the collection from [Truora's public workspace](https://www.postman.com/truora-api-docs/workspace/truora-api-docs/overview) within Postman: Import the truora collection .json: ### My Wordsáces > Import > Truora Collection.postman_collection.json ### Step 2: To execute requests, you'll need Truora account and supply your [API Key](https://dev.truora.com/guides/authentication/index.html), then set it as an environment variable within your workspace: ### create a new environment > put variable name > Add your secret > save then select the new environment you just created: If your environment is set up correctly, you should see the value of your secret key if you hover over the secret_key variable in the Token field: ### Everything is ready to start making a test call!! You are now ready to make a test call. An easy first call is to create Enrollment for an email validation: Add parameters to the call by clicking the Body tab, where you will see a list of available parameters. Select and fill in the required fields: If your environment is set up you'll get a object back as the response to the call:

request.description: Returns the results of the check that matches the ID provided, complete with a set of scores explained below. ### Scores: - **Global Score**: Average risk associated with a person, company or vehicle, according to the background check results. The global score considers results that are validated with the ID number provided. The score ranges from 0 to 1, where 0 represents high risk and 1 low risk. - **ID Score**: Average risk associated with a person according to the background check results. The ID score considers the results that are validated with a person identity document. The score ranges from 0 to 1, where 0 represents high risk and 1 low risk. - **Name Score**: Average risk associated with a person according to the background check results. The name score considers results that are validated against the name of a person and could not be validated with their ID number. These results might have homonyms associated with them. The score ranges from 0 to 1, where 0 represents high risk and 1 low risk. In order to calculate these scores, a weighted average is considered with different weights allocated to each dataset. Scores can be customized using the config endpoints by assigning a weight to each dataset according to its relevance. Keep in mind that results from the API vary depending on the country, check type and the inputs entered on check creation. ### **Automated results through WebHooks** To get Background Check result automatically, you must subscribe webhooks. For more information, please see [Webhooks guide](https://dev.truora.com/guides/webhook_rule/index.html).

request.description: Create a [custom type](https://dev.truora.com/checks/custom_type/) selecting the weight for each background check dataset and the country where it applies. Weights are numbers between 0 and 1 that represent how impactful the dataset is for the score, where datasets with weight 0 do not influence the score but are searched nonetheless. **Keep in mind that** the sum of all weights must equal 1. To perform a check with the custom type, create a Check and enter the name you gave to your custom type in `type`

item.description: The Custom Type API allows the creation of custom searches so only desired datasets are included in background checks. Thus increasing the check efficiency. Additionally, the impact each dataset has in the global score can be customized by giving it a weight value between 0 and 1. **Keep in mind that** the sum of all weights must equal 1. You can use the custom types in your checks to perform custom-type checks. Check out our [**Guide on Custom types**](https://developer.truora.com/products/checks/creating_a_custom_type.html) to learn more.

item.name: Settings 🔧

urlencoded.description:
**Type:** enum
Defines the matching type between the names retrieved from the identity databases and the names found in the criminal, legal and international databases to determine whether a record should be included in the check or not. `soft` (used by default) means matching names when they are similar enough to be considered the same person (e.g., Maria Alejandra Gomez would match Alejandra Gomez). `exact` means the names must perfectly match. Keep in mind that this feature is a configuration at a Client level, so it will affect all your check types.

**Type:** boolean
Indicates whether or not database queries must be retried until they successfully return a response or until the `max_duration` time is reached.

**Type:** string
Indicates the maximum amount of time a check can take to fetch responses. It follows the following format `xt` where `x` is a number and `t` is a letter ( `m` for minutes or `h` for hours). Example `25m` indicates 25 minutes, `2h` indicates 2 hours. This value must be between 15 minutes and 7 days (168 hours). When not configured, it is set to `default`. If `retries` is enabled, the default max duration is set to 48 hours; otherwise, it is set to 3 hours for Colombia, Mexico, Peru, and Brazil; 48 hours for Chile and International searches; and 72 hours for Costa Rica.

Example `25m` indicates 25 minutes, `2h` indicates 2 hours. This value must be between 15 minutes and 7 days (168 hours). When not configured, it is set to `default`. If `retries` is enabled, the default max duration is set to 48 hours; otherwise, it is set to 3 hours for Colombia, Mexico, Peru, and Brazil; 48 hours for Chile and International searches; and 72 hours for Costa Rica.

This body parameter must be sent for each column you want to upload in the xlsx file, replacing the input_name with the name of the input (e.g. columns_mapping.national_id). The value must be the index of the column in the file, being 0 for column A, 1 for column B and so on. **[Optional]**
Time between background checks to create a batch of continuous checks. It can be daily, weekly, monthly, yearly or have a custom frequency written as a number accompanied by a letter d: day, w: week, m: month, y: year. For instance: 3d: every three days, 2w: every two weeks.
**Note:** If you want to create a batch of continuous checks it is mandatory to send both end_date and frequency body parameters.

request.description:
This endpoint facilitates the creation of batches of different types. This endpoint does not include input file uploading or batch start logic.

The check inputs to be uploaded in the file can be manually mapped using `columns_mapping.{input_name}` body params, by default the inputs accepted by the check type will be mapped. The request will return a `columns` array with the order in which the inputs should be in the xlsx file.

The batch creation request returns a temporary URL in the `file_upload_link` field. This URL must be used to make a _PUT_ request with the file containing the mapped batch data. **It is crucial to note that the URL has a limited expiration time (30min) and will only allow the first submitted file to be uploaded. Therefore, it is highly recommended to carefully review the information before uploading.**

urlencoded.description:
**Type:** `enum`
**[Required]** Indicates the type of validation to be performed. Set this value to `document-validation`.

**Type:** `boolean`
**[Required]** This indicates that you have the authorization of the person to be verified. This is mandatory in order to comply with data protection laws. Value must be set to `true` in order to perform the validation.

**Type:** `enum`
**[Required]** Set the country of the document being validated.
**Allowed:** `CO`, `CL`, `MX`, `PE`, `BR`, `CR`,`ALL`.
Use `ALL` when `document_type` is set to passport.
For `BR`,and `CR` please ask sales team.

**Type:** `enum`
**[Required]** Set this value to the type of document been validated. For  `ppt ` please ask sales team.
**Allowed** `national-id` | `foreign-id` | `driver-license` | `passport` | `identity-card`| `rut` | `ppt` | `invoice` | `picture-id` | `record` | `cnh`.

**Type:** `string`
Unique user identifier for tracking validations of a user.*

*Only Account IDs following the regex pattern [a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+ are supported. While optional, it’s recommended to provide a unique identifier for accurate monitoring and data isolation between users. Utilize a library for managing user identifiers (User ID) based on your programming language or development framework. Avoid using repetitive number sequences. If left empty, an automatic account_id will be generated. Please go to Create an [Account ID](https://dev.truora.com/guides/account_id/) to learn more about it.

**Type:** `string`
When specified, indicates the name of the **custom type check** when a background check linked to this validation is desired.**

** Every time you run a **document validation** our system runs a default **Background check** (no extra charge), which will only verify datasets related  to **identity** and excludes criminal, legal, international or any other dataset types. If you wish customize the default behavior, use this field to conduct a [Background Check](https://dev.truora.com/guides/understading_background_checks/) with a [Custom Type](https://dev.truora.com/guides/background_check_custom_type/).
  * **Note 1**: You must have enabled the background check (BGC) product to use **Custom Type**. For each document validation request with a custom background check, you will be charged for it with the BGC price.
  * **Note 2**: If you create a **Custom Type**, you must add the dataset ``dataset_document_validation`` key with a value of **zero**.
  * You will identify within the **Get Validation** response that a **check_id** has been created.
**Type:** `string` [**Required if you have active retries and want to retry a previous validation**]: Refers to the validation_id of the failed validation.

**Type:** `int` 
The time, in seconds, users will have to complete the validation. If omitted, the timeout from the client's config will be used. The default value is 300 seconds (5 minutes), with a maximum of 21600 seconds (6 hours).

The **Delete Config** request allows you to delete a validator's configuration or a specific parameter within the validator's configuration. Deleting the configuration resets its values back to their defaults.

**Note:** If you omit the `type` parameter when sending the request, all configurations associated with the validator will be deleted, reverting all values to their default settings.

To learn about specific configurable parameters, default values, and special considerations, please visit the [Validators Config guide](https://dev.truora.com/validators/config_validators/).

item.description:

When a validation is executed, these parameters override the default configuration we have stored for any validation performed in **Truora**, ensuring that customers have all models up to date. The document validation process involves receiving pictures of either one or both sides of a document, depending on the type of document and the country it's from. The pictures are then analyzed to confirm that the text can be accurately read through the attachment. The process begins by using Optical Character Recognition (OCR) to recognize the text, then we classify the document and label the information. Finally, the authenticity of identity documents is verified. We share all the process information extracted and the results of the validation.

Please see more details in the [Document Validation via API guide ](https://dev.truora.com/validators/document_validation_api/) and the [API Reference.](https://dev.truora.com/docs/#main-validator-suite-api-section)

**[Required]** Indicates the type of validation to be performed. Set this value to `face-recognition`.

**Type:** `boolean`
**[Required]** This indicates that you have the authorization of the person to be verified. This is mandatory in order to comply with data protection laws. Value must be set to `true` in order to perform the validation.

**Type:** `string`
Unique user identifier for tracking validations of a user.*
*Only Account IDs following the regex pattern [a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+ are supported. While optional, it’s recommended to provide a unique identifier for accurate monitoring and data isolation between users. Utilize a library for managing user identifiers (User ID) based on your programming language or development framework. Avoid using repetitive number sequences. If left empty, an automatic account_id will be generated. Please go to Create an [Account ID](https://dev.truora.com/guides/account_id/) to learn more about it.

**Type:** `enum`
**[Required]** Used to specify the creation of enrollment that does not require an identity validation to complete. Set as `none` to skip the required validation. If omitted, the enrollment will require the normal validation process to be successful.*
*Note 1: To use the `confirmation` parameter your company’s account must be authorized. To request authorization, please contact our Support Team. This authorization will affect only your Truora-API-Keys created after the approval.

*Note 2: If your company requires this functionality, read the next section [Enrollment without validation](https://dev.truora.com/validators/face_validation_api/#enrollment-without-validation-auth-required) to learn about this special process.

> - Please be aware that the Create Enrollment endpoint will not allow the creation of an enrollment for an account that already has an associated enrollment.

> - Enrollment status will initially be pending with the reason set to waiting_file_upload until the reference image is successfully uploaded in the next step. Once the image is uploaded, the status will remain pending, but the reason will change to waiting_face_validation.

> - The account_id, enrollment_id and file_upload_link returned by this endpoint will be used in subsequent steps.

request.description:
The reference image must be submitted through a `PUT` request to the URL returned in the `file_upload_link` field of the previous step `Create Enrollment`, selecting the **Binary** type in the **Body**. The image formats supported are: jpeg, png and webp. Maximum size is 10 MB.

Please see more details in the [Face Validation via API guide ](https://dev.truora.com/validators/face_validation_api/) and the [API Reference](https://dev.truora.com/docs/#main-validator-suite-api-section).

The **Get enrollment** request checks the `status` of the enrollment, which can be either **pending**, **success** or **failure**: **Pending Status** - The `status` starts at `pending` with the `reason` set to `waiting_file_upload`, and remains that way until the image is successfully uploaded. - After the upload, the `status` remains `pending`, but the `reason` changes to `waiting_face_validation`. **Success Status** - Once the face validation is successful (next steps), the enrollment `status` will change to `success`. **NOTE:** After a successful validation, no further enrollments can be performed with the same `account_id`. This is because the `account_id` will now have an associated successful enrollment with its `enrollment_id`. **Failure Status** - The enrollment `status` can also be `failure` if the enrollment is declined or due to an internal error. Here´s a complete reference of the reasons why an enrollment can be declined: | Reason | Explanation | | --- | --- | | `no_face_detected` | Indicates that no face has been detected in the image that will be used as a reference to compare the next photo or video. | | `face_in_blocklist` | Indicates that the face in the base image has been detected in the client's block list. [Click Here](https://dev.truora.com/guides/truface/) to learn more about **TruFace** block list. | | `file_format_not_supported` | Indicates that the format of the base image is not compatible with the system. Allowed formats: JPG, PNG, and JPEG. | | `face_validation_failed` | The performed `face-recognition` validation to confirm a match between the face in the reference image and the user's face was unsuccessful. | | `system_error` | An error occurred while processing the enrollment inputs. | **Note:** You must use the enrollment_id returned in step 1 (Create enrollment).

urlencoded.description:
**Type:** `enum`
**[Required]** Indicates the type of validation to be performed. Set this value to `face-recognition`.

**Type:** `boolean`
**[Required]** This indicates that you have the authorization of the person to be verified. This is mandatory in order to comply with data protection laws. Value must be set to `true` in order to perform the validation.

**Type:** `string`
Unique user identifier for tracking validations of a user.*
*Only Account IDs following the regex pattern [a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+ are supported. While optional, it’s recommended to provide a unique identifier for accurate monitoring and data isolation between users. Utilize a library for managing user identifiers (User ID) based on your programming language or development framework. Avoid using repetitive number sequences. If left empty, an automatic account_id will be generated. Please go to Create an [Account ID](https://dev.truora.com/guides/account_id/) to learn more about it.

**Type:** `Number`
The minimum required similarity between the base image and the facial recognition image or video to pass validation. By default, the threshold is set to 0.65.

**Type:** `Enum` Allowed: ``passive`` ┃ ``disabled``. Indicates whether liveness detection is to be used for the face validation. If is set to ``disabled`` or the parameter is omitted, the system will default to using a selfie for validation. * ***NOTE**: The parameter ``liveness`` is soon to be deprecated and will be replaced with the parameter ``subvalidations``. For more information, please visit the Facial Recognition Methods guide and/or **Contact Support**.

**Type:** `Array` Allowed: ``passive_liveness`` ┃ ``similarity`` | ``speech_match``. You can select more than one. Indicates which subvalidations you want to perform during face validation execution. If omitted, only ``similarity`` will be used. * ***NOTE**: The parameter ``liveness`` is soon to be deprecated and will be replaced with the parameter ``subvalidations``. For more information, please visit the Facial Recognition Methods guide and/or **Contact Support**.

**Type:** `String` [***Required if you want to retry a previous validation***]: Refers to the ``validation_id`` of the failed validation. Only available if **passive liveness** is enabled in ``subvalidations`` or ``liveness`` parameters. * ***NOTE**: The parameter ``liveness`` is soon to be deprecated and will be replaced with the parameter ``subvalidations``. For more information, please visit the Facial Recognition Methods guide and/or **Contact Support**.

**Type:** `enum`
[***Required for ``speech_match`` subvalidation***]: Allowed: ``very_high`` | ``high`` | ``medium`` | ``low``. Minimum precision that the audio transcription obtained from the video selfie should have with the speech_token.

**Type:** `Number`
The time, in seconds, users will have to complete the validation. If omitted, the timeout from the client's config will be used. The default value is 300 seconds (5 minutes), with a maximum of 21600 seconds (6 hours).

The amount of time, in seconds, that users will have to perform the validation before it times out. The default value is **300** seconds (5 minutes), with a maximum of **21600** seconds (6 hours) and a minimum of **1** second.

**Type:** ``integer``
The number of times the validation may be retried before failing. The default value value is **0 retries**, with a maximum of **2 retries**. **NOTE:** Retry feature is only available when **passive liveness** is enabled in the [Face Validation](https://dev.truora.com/guides/face_validation_api/) process.
For more information visit the [Facial Recognition Methods](dev.truora.com/guides/facial_recognition_methods/) guide.

**Type:** ``integer``
The time to live (TTL), in seconds, for creating a retry of any validation. The default and maximum value is **300 seconds** (5 minutes), with a minimum of **1 second**. **NOTE:** Retry feature is only available when **passive liveness** is enabled in the [Face Validation](https://dev.truora.com/guides/face_validation_api/) process.
For more information visit the [Facial Recognition Methods](dev.truora.com/guides/facial_recognition_methods/) guide.

**Type:** ``Boolean``
Determines whether manual review will be performed upon validation failure. You must contact Truora to activate this service. For more information please visit the [manual review](https://dev.truora.com/guides/manual_review/) guide.

**Type:** ``Number``
Determines the required similarity between the provided picture and video or selfie to pass the validation, ranging from **0 to 1**. Note that a higher value may increase friction with the end-user, resulting in decreased customer conversion, while a lower value might increase conversion but reduce the rigor of the validation. By default, the treshold is set to 0.65.

**Type:** ``Boolean``
When set to true, the system will check if the face uploaded for the validation is blocked in [TruFace](https://dev.truora.com/guides/truface/).

item.description:
The config feature allows you to customize various parameters to enhance or adjust the behavior of the **Face Validator** according to your company's needs.

The **Face Validator** has a default configuration, which you can leave as is or adjust to better suit your industry or use case.

The **Document Validator** has a default configuration, which you can leave as is or adjust to better suit your industry or use case. To learn about specific configurable parameters, default values, and special considerations, please visit the [Validators Config guide](https://dev.truora.com/validators/config_validators/).

Below are the default values for this configuration:

| Parameter | Default Value |
| --- | --- |
| timeout | 300 |
| allowed_retries | 0 |
| retry_ttl | 300 |
| use_manual_review | false |
| threshold | 0.65 |
| include_face_search \* | false |

**\*NOTE**: Using **TruFace**, you can block a face so it is rejected during the face recognition validation. For instructions in how to use TruFace, consult the [TruFace Guide](https://dev.truora.com/guides/truface/).

To enable **TruFace** for the validations you set the `include_face_search` config to `true`.

Facial Recognition verifies the identity of a person by comparing a video or selfie of the person with a reference picture of the person. Both must clearly display his or her face like a portrait.

In case of comparison with video there are one form, **Passive:** The person takes a motionless video for 3 seconds.

Please see more details in the [Face Validation via API guide ](https://dev.truora.com/validators/face_validation_api/) and the [API Reference.](https://dev.truora.com/docs/#main-validator-suite-api-section)

## Block and detect faces with TruFace

Using TruFace, you can block a face so it is rejected during the face recognition validation. For instructions in how to block a face, consult the [TruFace guide](https://dev.truora.com/validators/truface/).

To enable TruFace for the validations you create, use the `create validation config` or `update validation config` endpoints to set the `include_face_search` config to `true`.

**[Required]** Enter this field only if you wish to block a face. Indicates the reason to block a face.

**Allowed:** `manipulated_document` ┃ `manipulated_photo`┃ `non_payment` ┃ `criminal_records_in_company` ┃ `fraudster` ┃ `user_register`

- `manipulated_document`: Indicates that this person is known to use manipulated documents to pass identity validations.

- `manipulated_photo`: ndicates that this person is known to manipulate photos to pass identity validations.

- `non_payment`: indicates that this person has incomplete payments and refuses to pay.

- `criminal_records_in_company`: Indicates that this person has some sort of criminal history with your company, or an ally.

- `user_register`: Indicates the face belongs to an authorized user and thus blocks anyone else trying to impersonate them.

- `fraudster`: Indicates you know this person to be a fraudster for reasons different from the above.

item.description: Once the [flow has been created and published](https://dev.truora.com/guides/flows_web/index.html), a POST request must be made to generate a temporary API Key. This should be generated every time a user validation is performed. After generating the API key, use the following URL: `https://identity.truora.com/?token=` and set the API key to perform the validation process and share the link. Once the has been generated, it can be verified on the [https://jwt.io/](https://jwt.io/) website. It's important to identify the `process_id`, as you'll need it to check the status of the endpoint through the get result method. Please see more details in [API Reference](https://docs.identity.truora.com/#post-/v1/api-keys)

Please see more details in [API Reference](https://docs.identity.truora.com/#post-/v1/api-keys)

In this endpoint, we have two default variables which are _`.name`_ and _`.lastname`_, but you can add other variables according to your needs. To use the variables, you must follow the following steps:

1. Adding variables: Once you have defined the location where the variable will be placed, you can add it using the following format _`.user`_ (the term 'user' will be replaced with the name chosen for your variable).
2. Assigning a value to the variable: We add a new key, and the name must follow the following format _var.user_ (the term 'user' will be replaced with the name chosen for your variable). Then, you must assign a value to your key.
    

**Note:** After the outbound is created and approved, it can be used or sent multiple times.

item.description:
There are two different types of messages that can be used through WhatsApp Business, incoming messages **(inbound)** and outgoing messages **(outbound)**. To configure WhatsApp with our services and validators you must follow the following steps:

1. Create an account with Truora
2. [Have a business WhatsApp (WABA) account.](https://uploads-ssl.webflow.com/5b559a554de48fbcb01fd277/618d69bc5a468a173e71811f_20211026_%20WABA%20account%20-%20Client%20Guide%20(EN).pdf) (we will help you create it as part of your onboarding process).
3. [Create a WhatsApp flow](https://dev.truora.com/guides/flows/index.html) to guide your users through a sequence of steps to interact with them and collect desired information.
    

Please see more details in [API Reference](https://docs.identity.truora.com/#tag--WhatsApp)

Truora Validatior Suite API allows performing identity validations in a variety of ways. - **Document Validation**: validates that the document is real (meaning we can find it in government databases) and that it hasn't been manipulated by extracting information from a front and back picture. - **Facial Recognition**: verifies the identity of a person by comparing a video of the person with a picture of the person. Both must clearly display his or her face like a portrait. - **TruFace**: allows blocking faces of known or suspected fraudsters to stop them from finishing their onboarding process regardless of the ID they might provide. - **Email Validation**: verifies the ownership or permissions a person has over an email by comparing the code the person enters and the one that is mailed to the email address provided. - **Phone validation**: verifies the ownership a person has over a phone by comparing the code the person enters and the one that was sent to the phone. - **Electronic Signature**: provides a link for performing an electronic signature on a document online and validates whether the person signed the document or not. Do not forget, the **Account ID** is a user identifier that allows you to track the validations made. It is important to generate a unique identifier for each user who performs any type of validation, in order to avoid the possibility of information being crossed between users and to guarantee adequate monitoring for each one of them. ## Validation steps Most validations are performed by following these three simple steps: Enrollment must be used for validators except for documents. Check out our [**Guide on Validation steps**](https://developer.truora.com/products/digital-identity/validation_steps.html) to learn more.

The Electronic Signature Validator provides a link for performing an electronic signature on a document created in [**ZapSign**](https://zapsign.com.br/) online.

We have 2 types of integrations available for this validator:

- You can use it in your DI Web or WhatsApp flows.
- You can integrate it via API.
    

The steps to follow for each type of integration are show below.

- **DI Web or WhatsApp flows:**
    - You must create an integration between Truora and Zapsing.
    - You must create a [Web](https://dev.truora.com/guides/zapsign_web_flow/index.html) or [WhatsApp](https://dev.truora.com/guides/zapsign_wa_flow/index.html) DI flow.
- **API Integration:**
    - If you want to do the electronic signature integration via API, please review the [ZapSign documentation](https://docs.zapsign.com.br/v/english/).

item.description:
An Outbound message is displayed when the company seeks to establish communication with its users.

Please see more details in [API Reference](https://docs.identity.truora.com/#get-/v1/whatsapp/outbounds)

WhatsApp can be used as a channel to reach users through flows. Using WhatsApp Business Account (WABA), you would be able to send and receive messages through the WhatsApp Business API for your business processes, interactions with your users or create validation processes, accounts as a document, document + face, email, phone number, electronic signature, and background. There are two different types of messages that can be used through WhatsApp Business, incoming messages **(inbound)** and outgoing messages **(outbound)**. To configure WhatsApp with our services and validators you must follow the following steps:

1. Create an account with Truora
2. [Have a business WhatsApp (WABA) account.](https://uploads-ssl.webflow.com/5b559a554de48fbcb01fd277/618d69bc5a468a173e71811f_20211026_%20WABA%20account%20-%20Client%20Guide%20(EN).pdf) (we will help you create it as part of your onboarding process).
3. [Create a WhatsApp flow](https://dev.truora.com/guides/flows/index.html) to guide your users through a sequence of steps to interact with them and collect desired information.
    

If you need more information, go to the [API reference](https://docs.identity.truora.com/#get-/v1/processes/-process_id-/result).
